executive_summary_zh: 今天候选内容整体偏弱，仅有一条较有操作性的官方更新，主题集中在 npm 供应链安全而非 AI coding 本身。
唯一值得关注的是 GitHub 发布的 npm staged publishing 与新的安装来源白名单参数，这对依赖治理、CI 安装策略和内部包发布流程有直接影响。
executive_summary_en: The candidate set is weak today, with only one actionable official update and little directly about AI coding systems.

The only notable item is GitHub's npm update adding staged publishing and new install-source allowlist flags, which has direct implications for dependency governance, CI install policy, and internal package release workflows.

items.title: Staged publishing and new install-time controls for npm
items.author: Allison
items.summary_zh: GitHub 宣布 npm CLI 11.15.0 提供两项面向供应链安全的更新：staged publishing 正式可用，以及新增安装来源控制参数 --allow-file、--allow-remote、--allow-directory，用于补充已有的 --allow-git。整体方向是把包发布与安装阶段的信任边界做得更细。
items.summary_en: GitHub announced two supply-chain security updates in npm CLI 11.15.0: staged publishing is now generally available, and new install-source control flags --allow-file, --allow-remote, and --allow-directory complement the existing --allow-git flag. The overall direction is finer-grained trust boundaries for both package publishing and installation.
items.why_it_matters_zh: 虽然这不是 AI coding 直接进展，但对使用代理编程、自动依赖修改、CI 自动构建的团队很相关。随着代码代理越来越频繁地生成 package.json 变更、拉取依赖并触发发布流程，安装来源限制和分阶段发布可以降低恶意依赖、错误发布和内部包扩散风险。
items.why_it_matters_en: While not directly about AI coding, this matters for teams using coding agents, automated dependency edits, and CI-driven builds. As agents increasingly modify package manifests, pull dependencies, and trigger release workflows, install-source restrictions and staged publishing help reduce the blast radius of malicious dependencies, accidental releases, and unintended propagation of internal packages.
key_takeaways_zh: npm 开始把“能从哪里安装”变成显式策略，适合在 CI、受控构建环境和企业开发镜像中启用。
staged publishing 让包发布从一次性全量暴露，转向更可控的渐进式发布流程，适合高风险或高影响依赖。
key_takeaways_en: npm is making install provenance a first-class policy surface, which is useful for CI, locked-down build environments, and enterprise developer images.
Staged publishing shifts package release from an all-at-once exposure model to a more controlled progressive rollout, which is valuable for high-risk or high-impact dependencies.

top_items: Staged publishing and new install-time controls for npm
new_terms_zh: install source allowlist flags（安装来源白名单参数）
themes_zh: 开发工具链的供应链安全前移
面向自动化与代理执行环境的依赖安装约束
themes_en: Shifting supply-chain security earlier into the developer toolchain
Dependency installation constraints for automated and agent-driven environments
implications_zh: 如果团队正在引入 AI coding agent，应同步收紧 npm 安装来源策略，避免代理在构建过程中引入不受控来源。
内部平台团队可以评估将 staged publishing 纳入标准发布流程，尤其适用于核心 SDK、CLI 和共享库。
implications_en: Teams adopting AI coding agents should tighten npm install-source policies in parallel to prevent agents from pulling dependencies from uncontrolled origins during builds.
Platform teams should evaluate making staged publishing part of the default release process, especially for core SDKs, CLIs, and shared libraries.
reading_order: Staged publishing and new install-time controls for npm
what_to_ignore_zh: 不要把这条更新误读为 AI coding 能力发布；它更偏向基础设施与供应链控制。
不必关注 GitHub Changelog 的归档页本身，只有具体 npm 更新条目有价值。
what_to_ignore_en: Do not interpret this as an AI coding capability launch; it is primarily an infrastructure and supply-chain control update.
Ignore the GitHub Changelog archive page itself; only the specific npm update entry is worth reading.
